                      (⨆ p ∈ (I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p c : Submodule K (M.obj c)) = (⨆ p ∈ (I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p c : Submodule K (M.obj c)) := rfl
                      _ = ⊤ := by
                        -- Use the fact that the supremum of the set is the top element to conclude that the supremum of the pointwise images is also the top element.
                        have h₁₀ : (⨆ p ∈ (I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p c : Submodule K (M.obj c)) = ⊤ := by
                          -- Use the property of the direct sum decomposition to get the top element.
                          simpa using DirectSumDecomposition.pointwise_iSup_eq_top I c
                        exact h₁₀
                  exact h₉
                exact h₈
          exact h₇
        rw [h₅]
      exact h₃
    -- Combine the results to show the final equality
    have h₃ : ⨆ (p : I), ↑p.val c = ⊤ := by
      rw [h₁]
      exact h₂
    -- Show that the supremum of the submodules is the top submodule
    simpa using h₃

have h_main : ⨆ p : I, (p : PersistenceSubmodule M) c = ⊤ := by
    have h₁ : (sSup (SetLike.coe I)) = ⊤ := I.sSup_eq_top'
    have h₂ : (sSup (SetLike.coe I)) c = (⊤ : C → Submodule K (M _)) c := by rw [h₁]
    have h₃ : (sSup (SetLike.coe I)) c = ⨆ p ∈ (SetLike.coe I), p c := by
      simp [sSup_apply]
      <;>
      congr 1 <;>
      ext <;>
      simp [SetLike.mem_coe]
      <;>
      aesop
    have h₄ : (⊤ : C → Submodule K (M _)) c = ⊤ := by simp
    have h₅ : ⨆ p ∈ (SetLike.coe I), p c = ⊤ := by
      calc
        ⨆ p ∈ (SetLike.coe I), p c = (sSup (SetLike.coe I)) c := by rw [h₃]
        _ = (⊤ : C → Submodule K (M _)) c := by rw [h₂]
        _ = ⊤ := by simp [h₄]
    have h₆ : ⨆ p : I, (p : PersistenceSubmodule M) c = ⨆ p ∈ (SetLike.coe I), p c := by
      simp [SetLike.mem_coe]
      <;>
      congr 1 <;>
      ext <;>
      simp_all [SetLike.coe]
      <;>
      aesop
    calc
      ⨆ p : I, (p : PersistenceSubmodule M) c = ⨆ p ∈ (SetLike.coe I), p c := by rw [h₆]
      _ = ⊤ := by rw [h₅]
  sorry

-- Prove that the pointwise supremum of the submodules in the direct sum decomposition is the entire space
  have h : ⨆ (p : I), (p : I).val c = ⊤ := by
    have h₁ : ⨆ (p : I), (p : I).val c = ⨆ (p : I), (p : I).val c := rfl
    rw [h₁]
    -- Use the property of pointwise supremum in a direct sum decomposition
    have h₂ : ⨆ (p : I), (p : I).val c = ⊤ := by
      -- The pointwise supremum of the direct sum decomposition is the entire space
      have h₃ : ⨆ p ∈ (Set.univ : Set I), (p : I).val c = ⊤ := by
        apply pointwise_sSup_eq_top
        <;>
        (try unfoldIcc) <;>
        (try simp_all [pointwise_sSup_eq_top]) <;>
        (try assumption) <;>
        (try infer_instance) <;>
        (try aesop)
      -- Convert the supremum over a set to the supremum over all elements
      have h₄ : ⨆ (p : I), (p : I).val c = ⨆ p ∈ (Set.univ : Set I), (p : I).val c := by
        simp [iSup]
        <;>
        (try aesop)
      rw [h₄] at *
      exact h₃
    exact h₂
  simpa [iSup] using h

have h₁ : sSupIndep (↑I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := I.sSupIndep'
    have h₂ : ∀ (s : Set I), sSupIndep (Set.range (fun p : s ↦ (p.val : I).val)) := by
      intro s
      have h₃ : sSupIndep (Set.range (fun p : s ↦ (p.val : I).val)) := by
        -- Use the fact that the original set is supremum independent
        have h₄ : Set.range (fun p : s ↦ (p.val : I).val) ⊆ (↑I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := by
          intro x hx
          rcases hx with ⟨p, rfl⟩
          exact I.mem_coe p.prop
        -- Apply the subset property of supremum independence
        have h₅ : sSupIndep (Set.range (fun p : s ↦ (p.val : I).val)) := by
          apply sSupIndep.mono h₄ h₁
        exact h₅
      exact h₃
    -- Convert the statement to the required form
    have h₃ : ∀ (s : Finset I), Set.InjOn (fun p : s ↦ (p.val : I).val) (↑s : Set s) →
        LinearIndependent K (fun p : s ↦ ((p.val : I).val c : Submodule K (M obj c))) := by
      intro s h_inj
      -- Use the fact that the original set is supremum independent
      have h₄ : sSupIndep (Set.range (fun p : s ↦ (p.val : I).val)) := h₂ s
      -- Convert the supremum independence to linear independence
      have h₅ : LinearIndependent K (fun p : s ↦ ((p.val : I).val c : Submodule K (M obj c))) := by
        -- Use the fact that the original set is supremum independent
        have h₆ : LinearIndependent K (fun p : s ↦ ((p.val : I).val c : Submodule K (M obj c))) := by
          -- Apply the pointwise linear independence
          have h₇ : ∀ (p q : s), p ≠ q → Disjoint ((p.val : I).val c) ((q.val : I).val c) := by
            intro p q hpq
            have h₈ : Disjoint (p.val : I).val (q.val : I).val := by
              -- Use the fact that the original set is supremum independent
              have h₉ : sSupIndep (↑I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := I.sSupIndep'
              have h₁₀ : Set.InjOn (fun x : I ↦ (x : I)) (↑I : Set I) := by
                intro x _ y _ hxy
                simpa using hxy
              have h₁₁ : Disjoint (p.val : I).val (q.val : I).val := by
                -- Use the fact that the original set is supremum independent
                have h₁₂ := h₉
                simp_all [SetLike.ext_iff, Disjoint]
                <;> aesop
              exact h₁₁
            -- Use the fact that the original set is supremum independent
            have h₉ : Disjoint ((p.val : I).val c) ((q.val : I).val c) := by
              apply h₈
            exact h₉
          -- Use the fact that the original set is supremum independent
          have h₈ : LinearIndependent K (fun p : s ↦ ((p.val : I).val c : Submodule K (M obj c))) := by
            -- Apply the pointwise linear independence
            apply linearIndependent_subtype_of_disjoint _ _ h₇
          exact h₈
        exact h₆
      exact h₅
    -- Use the pointwise linear independence to prove the statement
    have h₄ : IsIndependent K (fun p : I ↦ (p.val c : Submodule K (M obj c))) := by
      -- Use the fact that the original set is supremum independent
      rw [IsIndependent]
      intro s _
      have h₅ := h₃ s (by
        intro p _ q _ h
        simp_all [Subtype.ext_iff]
      )
      exact h₅
    exact h₄
  · -- Prove the generation part
    have h₁ : ⨆ p : I, (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) = ⊤ := by
      have h₂ : sSup (↑I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) = ⊤ := I.sSup_eq_top'
      -- Convert the supremum to the required form
      have h₃ : ⨆ p : I, (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) = sSup (↑I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := by
        simp [iSup_coe]
      rw [h₃]
      exact h₂
    -- Use the fact that the supremum is the entire space
    have h₂ : ⨆ p : I, (p.val c : Submodule K (M obj c)) = ⊤ := by
      have h₃ : (⨆ p : I, (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M)) c = ⊤ := by
        have h₄ : (⨆ p : I, (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M)) = ⊤ := h₁
        rw [h₄]
        simp
      -- Use the fact that the supremum is the entire space
      have h₄ : (⨆ p : I, (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M)) c = ⨆ p : I, (p.val c : Submodule K (M obj c)) := by
        simp [iSup_coe]
      rw [h₄] at h₃
      exact h₃
    exact h₂

· -- We need to prove that the supremum of the images is the entire space.
    -- This follows from the fact that the carrier set's supremum is ⊤.
    have h : ⨆ (p : I), (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) c = ⊤ := by
      -- Use the property that the supremum of the carrier set is ⊤
      have h₁ : (⨆ (p : I), (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) c) = ⊤ := by
        -- Since the supremum of the carrier set is ⊤, the supremum of the images is ⊤
        have h₂ : sSup (SetLike.coe I) = ⊤ := I.sSup_eq_top'
        -- Use the fact that the supremum of the images is the same as the image of the supremum
        have h₃ : (⨆ (p : I), (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) c) = (sSup (SetLike.coe I)) c := by
          -- Use the definition of supremum for the carrier set
          simp [SetLike.coe]
          <;>
          simp_all [iSup_subtype]
          <;>
          congr 1 <;> ext <;> simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
          <;>
          aesop
        -- Substitute the supremum of the carrier set into the equation
        rw [h₃, h₂]
        <;>
        simp [top_mem]
      exact h₁
    -- Use the fact that the supremum of the images is ⊤ to conclude the proof
    simpa using h
is in the lattice of submodules

- For any `p : { p : PersistenceSubmodule M | p ∈ I.carrier }`, `(p : PersistenceSubmodule M) c = p.val c`.
  - Therefore, `⨆ p : { p : PersistenceSubmodule M | p ∈ I.carrier }, (p : PersistenceSubmodule M) c` is exactly `⨆ p ∈ I.carrier, p c`, because the subtype is exactly `I.carrier`.
  - But `(sSup I.carrier) c = ⊤ c` (because `sSup I.carrier = ⊤`).
  - But `(sSup I.carrier) c = ⨆ p ∈ I.carrier, p c` (by definition of `sSup` for `PersisteceSubmodule M`).
  - Therefore, `⨆ p ∈ I.carrier, p c = ⊤`, as desired.

However, it is tricky to translate this into Lean, because Lean uses `sSup` internally to define `⨆` (as a special case).

Alternatively, perhaps we should use the fact that `⨆ p ∈ I.carrier, p c = ⊤`, and recognize that `⨆ p, ↑p c` is the same as `⨆ p ∈ I.carrier, p c`.

But perhaps in Lean:
   - `⨆ p : { p : PersistenceSubmodule M | p ∈ I.carrier }, (p : PersistenceSubmodule M) c = ⨆ p

have h₁ : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe I) := (I.sSupIndep')
    have h₂ : Set.InjOn (fun p : I ↦ (p : PersistenceSubmodule M)) (Set.univ : Set I) := by
      intro x _ y _ h
      exact Subtype.ext (by simpa using h)
    simpa [h₂] using h₁.image (Set.univ : Set I) (fun p : I ↦ (p : PersistenceSubmodule M))
  · -- Prove the iSup_eq_top part
    have h₃ : ⨆ p ∈ Set.univ, (p : I).val c = ⊤ := by
      have h₄ : ⨆ p ∈ (I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p c = ⊤ := (I.sSup_eq_top')
      have h₅ : (Set.univ : Set I) = Set.univ := rfl
      calc
        ⨆ p ∈ Set.univ, (p : I).val c = ⨆ p ∈ Set.univ, (p : I).val c := rfl
        _ = ⨆ p ∈ (I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p c := by
          -- We need to show that the supremum over all p in I of p c is the same as the supremum over all p in the carrier of I of p c.
          -- This is because the carrier of I is exactly the set of p in I.
          simp [SetLike.coe_setOf]
          <;>
          apply iSup_congr
          <;>
          simp_all [SetLike.mem_coe]
          <;>
          aesop
        _ = ⊤ := by rw [h₄]
    simpa using h₃

  lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ↦ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor
  · -- Prove the sSupIndep part
    have h₁ : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe I) := (I.sSupIndep')
    have h₂ : Set.InjOn (fun p : I ↦ (p : PersistenceSubmodule M)) (Set.univ : Set I) := by
      intro x _ y _ h
      exact Subtype.ext (by simpa using h)
    simpa [h₂] using h₁.image (Set.univ : Set I) (fun p : I ↦ (p : PersistenceSubmodule M))
  · -- Prove the iSup_eq_top part
    have h₃ : ⨆ p ∈ Set.univ, (p : I).val c = ⊤ := by
      have h₄ : ⨆ p ∈ (I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p c = ⊤ := (by
        have h₅ : ⨆ p ∈ (I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p c = ⊤ := by
          have h₆ : sSup (SetLike.coe I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) = ⊤ := I.sSup_eq_top'
          have h₇ : ⨆ p ∈ (I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p c = (sSup (SetLike.coe I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))) c := by
            simp [iSup_coe_sort]
          rw [h₇]
          simpa [h₆] using congr_arg (fun x => x c) (by simp [I.sSup_eq_top'])
        exact h₅
      )
      have h₅ : (Set.univ : Set I) = Set.univ := rfl
      calc
        ⨆ p ∈ Set.univ, (p : I).val c = ⨆ p ∈ Set.univ, (p : I).val c := rfl
        _ = ⨆ p ∈ (I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p c := by
          -- We need to show that the supremum over all p in I of p c is the same as the supremum over all p in the carrier of I of p c.
          -- This is because the carrier of I is exactly the set of p in I.
          simp [SetLike.coe_setOf]
          <;>
          apply iSup_congr
          <;>
          simp_all [SetLike.mem_coe]
          <;>
          aesop
        _ = ⊤ := by rw [h₄]
    simpa using h₃
